# RACI Matrix Template

- **Project name:**
- **Project manager:**

- **Version:**
- **Last updated:**

---

## 1. RACI Matrix Overview
### 1.1 Purpose
Define roles and responsibilities for project activities, decisions, and deliverables to ensure
accountability and avoid confusion.

### 1.2 RACI Definitions
- **R - Responsible:** Performs the work or makes the decision.
- **A - Accountable:** Ultimately answerable for completion and quality.
- **C - Consulted:** Provides input and expertise (two-way communication).
- **I - Informed:** Kept up to date (one-way communication).

### 1.3 RACI Rules
- Every activity must have at least one **R**.
- Every activity must have exactly one **A**.
- Minimize the number of **C** assignments to avoid delays.
- Use **I** sparingly to avoid information overload.

---

## 2. Project Roles and Stakeholders
### 2.1 Key Roles
| Role | Name | Primary Responsibilities |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Project Sponsor |  | Strategic oversight, resource approval, issue escalation |
| Project Manager |  | Day-to-day management, planning, coordination, reporting |
| Technical Lead |  | Technical decisions, architecture, development oversight |
| Business Analyst |  | Requirements gathering, process analysis, documentation |
| QA Manager |  | Quality planning, testing oversight, defect management |
| Team Lead |  | Team coordination, task assignment, progress tracking |
| Developer |  | Code development, unit testing, implementation |
| End User Representative |  | User requirements, acceptance testing, feedback |
| IT Operations |  | Infrastructure, deployment, production support |
| Business Owner |  | Business requirements, acceptance, change approval |

### 2.2 External Stakeholders
| Role | Organization | Contact | Key Involvement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Vendor Representative |  |  | Technical support, integration |
| Compliance Officer |  |  | Regulatory approval, audit |
| Security Team |  |  | Security review, approval |

---

## 6. RACI Guidelines & Best Practices
### 6.1 Creating Effective RACI Matrices
1. Start with activities and decisions first.
2. Include all relevant roles and stakeholders.
3. Assign responsibilities using consistent RACI definitions.
4. Validate the matrix with the team.
5. Update as the project evolves.

### 6.2 Common RACI Mistakes to Avoid
- Too many As (only one accountable).
- No Rs (every activity needs a responsible).
- Excessive Cs (slows decisions).
- Unclear role definitions.
- Stale matrices that are never updated.

### 6.3 RACI Validation Checklist
- [ ] Each activity has exactly one A (Accountable).
- [ ] Each activity has at least one R (Responsible).
- [ ] Consulted roles are limited to essential input.
- [ ] Informed roles are relevant and necessary.
- [ ] The team understands and agrees with assignments.

---

## 7. Communication & Implementation
### 7.1 RACI Communication Plan
1. Draft review with key stakeholders.
2. Facilitate a team workshop to refine assignments.
3. Obtain formal approval from the sponsor.
4. Distribute the final matrix to all stakeholders.
5. Brief new team members during onboarding.

### 7.2 Monitoring & Updates
- Weekly check-ins to confirm roles are followed.
- Resolve role conflicts immediately.
- Update the matrix during phase transitions or staffing changes.
